title: Prototype-to-Production Pipeline
summary: Package scientific methods into reproducible, validated, and releasable services.
peek: Versioned methods, automated checks, release states, and repeatable packaging.

details.problem: Scientific methods often remain trapped in notebooks or one-off execution environments, which slows reuse and governance.
details.solution: Use automated packaging, release validation, and version tagging so a method can move from development to production without losing traceability.

title: Model Serving Infrastructure
summary: Expose scientific scoring and analytical methods through stable, versioned services.
peek: Containerized inference, method routing, and environment-specific release controls.

details.problem: A method that cannot be reliably served cannot become part of a durable scientific platform.
details.solution: Use containerized services with version-aware routing, health checks, and controlled promotion between environments.

title: API Contract Layer
summary: Define clear request and response contracts for scientists, applications, and autonomous tools.
peek: Schemas, documentation, trace IDs, and backward-compatible integration patterns.

details.problem: Without explicit contracts, integrations become brittle and difficult to govern.
details.solution: Use versioned API contracts with schema validation, trace identifiers, and consistent response envelopes.

title: Observability Guardrails
summary: Monitor service health, data integrity, drift, and release quality in one operational layer.
peek: Structured logging, latency tracking, drift checks, and release health monitoring.

details.problem: Operational visibility is required to trust scientific services in recurring workflows.
details.solution: Collect structured telemetry across inputs, outputs, latency, drift, and release status to support safe operation and incident response.

title: Scientist Workflow Surface
summary: Provide a simple path from dataset selection to interpretable results and exportable artifacts.
peek: Structured inputs, reproducibility metadata, and concise explanation surfaces.

details.problem: Scientific users need a direct workflow that does not require platform expertise to obtain reliable results.
details.solution: Offer a lightweight interface that supports dataset selection, parameter control, result review, and artifact export.

title: Agent-Ready Consumption Layer
summary: Allow autonomous systems to discover, call, and monitor scientific methods through governed interfaces.
peek: Tool manifests, scoped permissions, response contracts, and execution logging.

details.problem: Autonomous systems need machine-readable interfaces and governance boundaries before they can use scientific methods safely.
details.solution: Publish tool manifests, require contract compliance, scope permissions, and store execution traces for replay and audit.
